title: OpenClaw should suppress NO_REPLY output even when trailing text leaks

source.license_note: Localized ACBench reproduction derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #63054.

task.summary: Repair silent-reply detection so NO_REPLY-prefixed output is suppressed even when leaked trailing text follows it.
task.instructions: Investigate why NO_REPLY plus trailing leaked reasoning is still delivered to users, repair the silent-reply detection path, and preserve the existing exact-match and normal visible-text behavior.
task.acceptance_notes: Alternative fixes are allowed as long as leaked NO_REPLY-prefixed output is not delivered.

visible_context.issue_text: A provider can emit `NO_REPLY` followed immediately by leaked reasoning text, and OpenClaw sends the whole blob to the user instead of suppressing it.
error_logs: telegram delivery emitted 7 raw messages containing repeated NO_REPLY tokens
stopReason=length while leaked NO_REPLY-prefixed output kept streaming
reproduction_steps: Run the unit tests from the repository root.
visible_context.notes: This fixture focuses on silent-reply detection, not the full provider runtime.

code_fault.description: Silent reply detection only accepts exact NO_REPLY matches, allowing leaked trailing text to reach users.

constraints.notes: This GitHub-derived reproduction is expected to be repaired in code, not by changing tests.
